name: Kenyon Review Young Writers

prior_knowledge: Passion for literature and creative writing

essays.prompt: Submit a writing sample and answer: What are you reading right now, and what do you love about it?
tips: Kenyon values close reading and literary sensibility — show your reading life, not just your writing

outcomes.college_impact: Positive signal at top liberal arts colleges and creative writing programs. Kenyon Review itself is one of the most prestigious literary journals in the world.
outcomes.typical_results: Many alumni attend Kenyon or other top liberal arts colleges; strong literary community connections.

what_they_look_for: Literary sensibility and close reading ability

blurbs.short: A 2-week residential writing workshop at Kenyon College for talented young writers, centered on the Kenyon Review literary journal.
blurbs.long: Kenyon Review Young Writers is one of the most respected writing workshops for high school students, held at Kenyon College in Ohio. Kenyon Review is one of the top three literary journals in America (alongside The Paris Review and Granta), and the Young Writers program is taught by Kenyon College faculty and Kenyon Review editors. The program strikes a balance between generative writing and literary analysis — students both write and study literature. The setting (Kenyon College's rural campus) creates an intimate, focused atmosphere. Like Iowa, Kenyon has a deep literary culture — it publishes The Kenyon Review, hosts the Kenyon Review Podcast, and has a long tradition of literary excellence. The program is small (32 students) and selective.

curriculum: Daily writing workshops, craft seminars (close reading, literary analysis), literary readings, individual conferences, time for independent writing.
